page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by Intel processors running on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 dedicated physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is managed via the Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

This server configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, featuring 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. This local instance store offers high-speed, low-latency data access directly attached to the physical host, though it is ephemeral by nature.

Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 67 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers. Given the combination of 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, the m8idb.16xlarge is designed for workloads requiring a balance of compute and memory alongside fast local storage. Suitable applications include relational databases, caching layers, data analytics, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.16xlarge is a general-purpose, x86_64 architecture server powered by Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration is bundled with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, delivering high-speed local data access. With a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps, this server offers a balanced resource profile suitable for high-throughput workloads. The combination of dedicated compute resources, substantial memory, and high-capacity local storage makes it well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring low-latency storage access.
